refined_fr.refined_fr: Ensure the teleoperation link availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target to maintain continuous remote control capability.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; refining it to focus on achieving and maintaining the required link availability clarifies the performance target and scope for design.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Detect and recover from link failures promptly
Monitor link quality and report status

candidate_dps: Redundant network link architecture with failover
Real-time link health monitoring and diagnostics
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant network link architecture with failover

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with network hardware
Failover logic dependent on network link status
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability concerns and supports modular failover mechanisms, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased complexity impacting maintainability
High safety criticality requires robust failover without false triggers
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating link health monitoring DP explicitly in next refinement level to address detection and recovery.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link availability including network link redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections supporting teleoperation data exchange and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Reporting link status and health metrics to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands dependent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of link availability logic and low diffusion risk due to centralized failover management.
Vehicle module depends on link status but does not contain availability logic; higher diffusion risk if assigned here.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not manage link availability; assigning DP here would increase diffusion.
NGHE Solution oversees system but assigning link availability DP here risks diffusion across multiple modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability DP in Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and aligns ownership with functional responsibility.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module for physical network interfaces
Coordination required with Safety Services for alerting on link failures
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts and responsibilities between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to reduce cross-module dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping to link availability requirements and supports verification.

diffusion_risks: Minor diffusion risk if link monitoring logic spreads into Vehicle or Safety Services modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assignment to Teleoperation module provides strong containment and ownership clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics reported via Telemetry interface from Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility for link availability to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine sub-FRs to explicitly address link monitoring and recovery in next decomposition level.
Define detailed interface specifications between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and explicit DP definitions for monitoring and recovery are needed to fully address the high safety criticality and complexity.
next_level_sub_frs: Detect and recover from link failures promptly
Monitor link quality and report status

revise.why: To explicitly capture detection and recovery functions and select corresponding DPs for comprehensive coverage of link availability.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover
Teleoperation module can implement required failover logic
open_risks: Complexity of failover mechanisms impacting reliability
Interface dependencies between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules
evidence_needed: Performance data on network link redundancy and failover effectiveness
Verification plans for link availability metrics
